Sample Description

library(tidyverse)
library(reactable)
library(haven)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)

intro

Goal of this document is to create a description for every graph used in our analysis, this consists of:
- Itemname & Itemlabel
- M, SD for metric variables
- NA percentages
- Frequency/distribution of wanswer

loading

# dat_raw <- readRDS("../data/ESS_germany_selection.RDS")
dat_raw <- readRDS("../data/ESS_germany_v02.RDS") ### has to be run after running script 04an, if file does not exist

custom function

create_descriptive_plot <- function(data, item_col, 
                                    item_label,
                                    is_metric = TRUE, 
                                    show_names = TRUE, 
                                    rotate_x = 0,
                                    v_adjust = -0.2,
                                    h_adjust = 0.5,
                                    text_size = 3
                                    ) {
  
  # 1. Extract Label/Question Text
  frage_text <- attr(data[[item_col]], "label")
  if (is.null(frage_text)) frage_text <- item_col 
  frage_text_wrapped <- stringr::str_wrap(frage_text, width = 60)
  
  # 2. Stats for Subtitle
  raw_values <- data[[item_col]]
  na_perc    <- mean(is.na(raw_values)) * 100
  n_val      <- sum(!is.na(raw_values))
  
  stat_text <- if(is_metric) sprintf("M = %.2f, SD = %.2f, ", mean(raw_values, na.rm = TRUE), sd(raw_values, na.rm = TRUE)) else ""
  
  plot_title <- paste0(item_label, ": ", frage_text_wrapped)
  plot_subtitle <- sprintf("Variable: %s  |  %sN = %d, Fehlend = %.1f%%", item_col, stat_text, n_val, na_perc)
  
  # 3. Data Prep (Mapping for X-Axis Labels)
  # Using haven::as_factor to preserve the specific labels for missing codes
  mapping_df <- data.frame(
    val = raw_values,
    lab = as.character(haven::as_factor(raw_values))
  ) %>% 
    distinct() %>% 
    # Logic: Only add "Value - " prefix if it's not a missing code/text-only label
    mutate(
      val_lab = ifelse(!is.na(val) & as.character(val) != lab, 
                       paste0(val, " - ", lab), 
                       lab)
    )

  # 4. Plotting
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = haven::as_factor(.data[[item_col]]))) +
    geom_bar(fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
    geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = after_stat(count)), 
              vjust = v_adjust, hjust = h_adjust, size = text_size, angle = rotate_x) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) + 
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = plot_title, subtitle = plot_subtitle, x = "Antwortoption", y = "Häufigkeit") +
    
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) {
      if (show_names) {
        # Match back to our custom "1 - Label" string
        final_label <- mapping_df$val_lab[match(x, mapping_df$lab)]
        # If match fails (can happen with weird NAs), fall back to x
        if(all(is.na(final_label))) final_label <- x
      } else {
        final_label <- x
      }
      return(stringr::str_wrap(final_label, width = 15))
    }) +
    
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey30"),
      axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 8, angle = rotate_x, 
                                   hjust = if(rotate_x != 0) 1 else 0.5,
                                   vjust = if(rotate_x != 0) 1 else 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(p)
}

dependent variables

trust in politics

# 1. Variablen definieren
item_col <- "political_trust"
frage_text <- "Index aus 6 Variablen (z.B. Vertrauen in Parlament, Parteien, Justiz...)"
bin_breite <- 0.5 # Hier kannst du die Breite der Balken für den Index steuern (z.B. 0.5 oder 1)

# 2. Metadaten & Statistiken
frage_text_wrapped <- stringr::str_wrap(frage_text, width = 60)
raw_values <- dat_raw[[item_col]] # Sicherer Zugriff via [[ ]]

mean_val <- mean(raw_values, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_val   <- sd(raw_values, na.rm = TRUE)
na_perc  <- mean(is.na(raw_values)) * 100
n_val    <- sum(!is.na(raw_values))

plot_title    <- paste0(item_col, ": ", frage_text_wrapped)
plot_subtitle <- sprintf("Variable: %s  |  M = %.2f, SD = %.2f, N = %d, Fehlend = %.1f%%", 
                         item_col, mean_val, sd_val, n_val, na_perc)

# 3. Plot-Erstellung (Histogramm-Logik für Index-Werte)
p <- ggplot(dat_raw, aes(x = .data[[item_col]])) +
  # geom_histogram statt geom_bar für metrische Index-Daten
  geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", color = "white", binwidth = bin_breite) +
  
  # Häufigkeitszahlen über den Bins
  stat_bin(binwidth = bin_breite, geom = "text", 
           aes(label = after_stat(count)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title    = plot_title,
    subtitle = plot_subtitle,
    x        = "Indexwert (Vertrauen)",
    y        = "Häufigkeit"
  ) +
  # Sorgt dafür, dass alle Zahlen auf der X-Achse (z.B. 0 bis 10) sichtbar sind
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 10, by = 1)) + 
  
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, color = "grey30"),
    axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p
## Warning: Removed 141 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_bin()`).
## Removed 141 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_bin()`).

  Variable <- "vertrauen_in_politik"

ggsave(p,
       filename = paste0("../output/Anhang_A_",Variable,".png"),
       device = "png",
       dpi = 300,
       width = 8,
       height = 5,
       units = "in")

central independent variable

age

p <- create_descriptive_plot(dat_raw, "agea", "Alter",show_names = FALSE, rotate_x = 90,v_adjust = .5,h_adjust = -0.1)
print(p)

Variable <- "alter"

ggsave(p,
       filename = paste0("../output/Anhang_A_",Variable,".png"),
       device = "png",
       dpi = 300,
       width = 8,
       height = 5,
       units = "in")
